title: Enumerations should include Int32 storage
description: Enumerations should have "Int32" storageAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?By default the storage type of anenumisInt32. In most cases it is not necessary to change this. In particular you will not achieve any performance gain by using a smaller data type (e.g.Byte) and may limit future uses.Noncompliant code exampleusing System; namespace MyLibrary { public enum Visibility : sbyte // Noncompliant { Visible = 0, Invisible = 1, } }Compliant solutionusing System; namespace MyLibrary { public enum Visibility { Visible = 0, Invisible = 1, } }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: using System; namespace MyLibrary { public enum Visibility : sbyte // Noncompliant { Visible = 0, Invisible = 1, } }
compliant: using System; namespace MyLibrary { public enum Visibility { Visible = 0, Invisible = 1, } }

logic.detection_conditions: Enum declaration with non-Int32 storage type (sbyte, byte, short, ushort, uint, long, ulong) in source code
logic.triggering_conditions: Source code containing enum declaration with explicit base type that is NOT int or Int32
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude test code, generated code, string literals, comments, and enums with default/int/Int32 storage
never_trigger_on: Enums without explicit base type (default Int32)
Enums with explicit int or Int32 base type
logic.reasoning: Detects enum declarations with non-default storage types (sbyte, byte, short, ushort, uint, long, ulong). By default, enums use Int32 storage, which is optimal for most cases. Using smaller types (byte, sbyte) doesn't improve performance and may limit future use. The rule scans the CompilationUnit source for enum declarations with non-Int32 base types. The regex pattern matches enum keyword followed by identifier, colon, and non-Int32 type, while excluding string contexts, comments, and logging statements.

should_trigger: public enum Visibility : sbyte { Visible = 0, Invisible = 1 }
public enum Status : byte { Active, Inactive }
public enum Priority : short { Low = 1, Medium = 2, High = 3 }
public enum LargeEnum : long { Value1, Value2 }
should_not_trigger: public enum Status : int { Active, Inactive }
public enum Priority : Int32 { Low = 1, Medium = 2, High = 3 }
public enum Color { Red, Green, Blue }
